Off Calendar Apr-02-2013 Continued To Apr-17-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Justin Gilbert for and with Melba Bellinger. Alicia Gamez specially appearing for Attorney Amy Harrington for William Morgan (Conservatee). Alex Weyand for and with Oneida Morgan-Hawley. Joel Blackman for Public Guardian. Brenda Morgan in Propria Persona. Joseph Melendez also present. Timothy Bonicci also present.

Joseph Melendez stated that he did not receive notice of Mr. Weyand's petition, confirmed his address and stated that he was willing to go forward. The Public Guardian is to determine whether a Special Needs Trust is to be established or if they are going to spend down. Brenda Morgan explained that Mr. Bonicci is a friend that has advanced funds to her and that there is only one assignment in the amount of $26,000.00. The Court advised Brenda Morgan that it needs to know what the agreement is with Mr. Bonicci and what the terms are. The Parties agreed to meet with Pang Li (Setllement Officer) in room 603. The Court excused the Public Guardian from participating in the settlement and the trial. Upon returning, Mr. Weyand informed the Court that they have a settlement conference scheduled for April 12, 2013 and a deadline of April 9, 2013 to report with regard to progress of exchanging information. Ms. Gamez stated that she will continue to appear to make sure her Client's share is not litigated and raised the issue costs and how they were to be paid. A list of all of the personal items and videos will be provided to all Parties. The Parties had no objection to an advance of $5,000.00 to Brenda Morgan for medical purposes. All matters were continued to April 17, 2013 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court wants all Parties to get all of the issues resolved with Ms. Li.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chien.

Petition Of Beneficiary Mellba C. Bellinger To Compel Trustee Oneida M. Morgan-Hawley To Account
Off Calendar Nov-06-2012 Continued To Jan-22-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joel Blackman for Public Guardian (Conservator for William Morgan). David Olick for Melba Bellinger (present). Richard Canatella in Propria Persona. Amy Harrington Court appointed Counsel for William Morgan. Justin Gilbert for and with Melba Bellinger (Petitioner). Alex Weyand for Oneida Morgon-Hawley (Trustee). Brenda Morgan in Propria Persona.

All Parties presented oral argument to support their positions with regard the matter being properly before the Probate Court or whether it should be before Judge Robertson, the issue of Mr. Morgan not being present at the settlement and his capacity to enter into the settlement, the difficiencies in the accounting, the funds not being in a blocked account, Mr. Canatella's fees, how the matter could be resolved, missing items, offset to Melba's share, etc. Mr. Weyand stated that the Trustee has authorized an advance to Brenda. Brenda and Mr. Weyand are to talk regarding the funds. Status on filing of the Petition for Approval of the First Account was set for December 18, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Petition to Compel Trustee to Account and the Petition for An Accounting by The Successor Trustee were continued to January 22, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The hearing date on the accounting petition is to be set for January 22, 2013.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition Of Beneficiary Mellba C. Bellinger To Compel Trustee Oneida M. Morgan-Hawley To Account
Off Calendar Oct-16-2012 Continued To Nov-06-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Alex Weyand for and with Oneida Morgan-Hawley (Trustee). Amy Harrington Court appointed Counsel for William Morgan (Conservatee and Beneficiary). Joel Blackman for Public Guardian (Conservator of William Morgan). Justin Gilbert for and with Melba Bellinger. Joseph Melendez (Benificiary) in Propria Persona. Brenda Morgan (Beneficiary) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Weyand stated that they have a preliminary accounting prepared which covers from 2008 forward, that they are going over some errors with the CPA (Darryl Dwyer) and that he will provide a supplemental to provide the exact date as to when the accounting will be completed. Counsel expressed concerns about the Trust administration, the amount of Attorney fees paid, Mr. Canatella's indication that he represented all of the Beneficiaries, Mr. Canatella's fees and actions taken by Mr. Canatella. Mr. Blackman stated that Mr. Canatella should be part of this matter and that he may file a petition to get back some (1/5) of Mr. Canatella's fees. Mr. Weyand provided case history, explained difficulties with selling and vacating the house, and stated that he can get an exact amount of the CPA's fees. Status on filing of the accounting was set for November 6, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. No further expenses are to be paid out of the Trust other than the CPA's fees which are not to exceed $5,000.00. Mr. Weyand is to bring the petition up to the Clerk for early setting. Today's matters were also continued to November 6, 2012. The Court was unable to address Brenda Morgan's request for a $10,000.00 distribution as it is not before the Court.

Judge: Mary Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
